tldr: Move from a managed media platform to raw object storage on Azure: cheaper per-GB and lower lock-in, but you take on transformations, CDN, and tagging yourself.
why_switch: You want lower per-GB storage costs and standard object-storage semantics
You're standardizing on Azure for the rest of your stack
You don't need on-the-fly transformations, or you're moving them in-house
You want geo-redundancy and tiered storage (hot/cool/archive)
You need WORM / immutability policies for compliance
export_method: Use Cloudinary's official Admin API to enumerate assets and download originals to a staging location — consult Cloudinary docs for the current pagination and download patterns. Avoid downloading transformed derivatives; preserve the master.
import_method: Upload to an Azure Blob Storage container using the official Azure SDK or AzCopy. Use container/path naming that mirrors your Cloudinary folder structure. Configure access tier (Hot/Cool/Archive) per asset based on access patterns. Consult the official Azure docs for current AzCopy syntax and authentication options.

concept_mapping.note: Each Azure storage account hosts multiple containers; map folders to containers or blob prefixes.
Use the public_id path as the blob name to preserve structure.
Azure Blob doesn't transform images — pair with Azure Functions, a CDN with image processing, or a third-party service.
Stand up a CDN in front of the container; consult Azure docs for current options and pricing.
SAS tokens grant time-limited access; verify scopes in official docs.
Not built into Blob Storage — integrate separately if needed.
Enable on the container; consult docs for retention behavior.

effort: 2-6 weeks depending on asset volume, transformation complexity to replace, and CDN setup.
gotchas: You lose Cloudinary's transformation pipeline — plan a replacement before cutover
Cloudinary egress for the migration may be metered
URL structure changes — every embed and link needs updating or redirecting
Azure Blob has its own pricing dimensions (storage tier, operations, egress, redundancy) — model carefully
Archive tier has rehydration latency and cost — don't put hot assets there
Choose redundancy (LRS/ZRS/GRS) deliberately; cross-region adds cost

email.subject: Migrating media from Cloudinary to Azure Blob Storage
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving media assets from Cloudinary to Azure Blob Storage to consolidate on Azure and cut storage cost. Plan: pull originals via Cloudinary's Admin API, upload via AzCopy preserving paths, stand up a CDN, and replace transformations with our own pipeline (or drop them where unused). Expect 2-6 weeks. I'll circulate a detailed cutover plan before we start.

Thanks
